theme: Lathe recipe as a counted instruction tape
title: Shoulder-Shadow Turning: Stiffness-Aware Cut Interleaving
strategies: trivial: mimic the checker's own always-feasible baseline (unit-depth passes, single finishing tool, descending-target order)

greedy: fully finish one band at a time in a precedence-safe order (descending target), always taking the deepest currently-legal pass -- ignores that finishing a small-x band early collapses the prefix-min stiffness needed by not-yet-cut large-x bands

strong: roughs the deep, chatter-sensitive (large axial distance) bands FIRST while the whole rod is still full-thickness (max stiffness), reserves the shallow/easy bands for later, and batches all finishing passes at the end to also minimize tool-changes
invalid: emits a pass whose depth exceeds its tool's legal range (or otherwise malformed output) -> infeasible
